@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kepler-845-tracking-kepler-adopters",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kepler: [Tracking] Kepler Adopters",
+    "description": "[Tracking] Kepler Adopters. This issue affects 3+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify kepler feature symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your kepler deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=kepler\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kepler -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors related to: [Tracking] Kepler Adopters"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Check current resource configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kepler resources:\n```bash\nkubectl get resource -A\nkubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>\n```\n### Which document would you like to be mentioned?\n\nThis is a proposal to add a process for tracking Kepler adopters.\n\nOnce there is enough interest in this issue, we could move to a formal process fo"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [Tracking] Kepler Adopters",
+        "description": "Fixes [kepler#845](https://github.com/sustainable-computing-io/kepler/issues/845)\n\nGomplate is used to loop through the data/adopters.yaml file and render this list using the templates/adopters.md template. \nTherefore, future additions can be done by users by adding to data/adopters.yaml and raising PRs. This process is also described in the contributing docs.\nI also wrote how that should be done through GitHub Actions in theory but could not test it yet.\n\nWould be lovely to add GitHub Actions f\n\nSee the fix PR for details: linked PR"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm [Tracking] Kepler Adopters is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kepler -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The root cause is: Fixes [kepler#845](https://github.com/sustainable-computing-io/kepler/issues/845)\n\nGomplate is used to loop through the data/adopters.yaml file and render this list using the templates/adopters.md template. \nTherefore, future additions can be done by users by adding to data/adopters.yaml and raising PRs. This process is also described in the contributing docs.\nI also wrote how that should be done . This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
